content_en:
Abu Hamid Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Ghazali at-Tusi, known in the West as Algazel, was born in 450 AH (1058 CE) in Tus, a city in Khorasan (present-day Iran). He is among the most influential thinkers in Islamic history, and his impact spans theology, philosophy, jurisprudence, mysticism, and ethics. His life followed a dramatic arc: from provincial student to the most celebrated intellectual in the Islamic world, through a crisis of faith that led him to abandon his prestigious position in Baghdad, and finally to a life of Sufi discipline and teaching.

Al-Ghazali received his early education in Tus and then in Jurjan before coming to Nishapur, where he studied under Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni — the greatest Shafi'i-Ash'ari theologian and jurist of the era. From al-Juwayni he received thorough training in both Shafi'i jurisprudence and Ash'ari kalam. After al-Juwayni's death in 478 AH, al-Ghazali came under the patronage of Nizam al-Mulk, the powerful Seljuk vizier, who appointed him to head the Nizamiyyah madrasa in Baghdad in 484 AH.

At the Nizamiyyah al-Ghazali reached the peak of worldly scholarly success. His lectures attracted enormous audiences and his reputation was unrivaled. But in 488 AH, at the height of his fame, he abandoned his position and left Baghdad — driven, as he later described in his spiritual autobiography Deliverance from Error (Al-Munqidh min ad-Dalal), by a profound spiritual crisis about the certainty of religious knowledge. He spent years in Syria and Palestine in Sufi retreat before eventually returning to teaching in Khorasan.

Al-Mustasfa min Ilm al-Usul was one of his later works, written after his return from spiritual retreat when he had integrated his earlier jurisprudential training with his deepened spiritual and philosophical perspectives. By this point he had also completed his devastating critique of philosophy (Tahafut al-Falasifah) and his masterwork of Islamic spirituality (Ihya' Ulum ad-Din). The Mustasfa represents the culmination of his thinking about the theoretical foundations of Islamic law, written with the depth and clarity of a master at the height of his powers. Al-Ghazali died in 505 AH (1111 CE).
content_id:
Abu Hamid Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Ghazali at-Tusi, yang dikenal di Barat sebagai Algazel, lahir pada tahun 450 H (1058 M) di Tus, sebuah kota di Khurasan (Iran sekarang). Ia termasuk di antara pemikir-pemikir paling berpengaruh dalam sejarah Islam, dan dampaknya mencakup teologi, filsafat, fikih, mistisisme, dan etika. Hidupnya mengikuti busur yang dramatis: dari pelajar daerah menjadi intelektual paling terkenal di dunia Islam, melalui krisis iman yang membuatnya meninggalkan posisi bergengsinya di Baghdad, dan akhirnya menuju kehidupan disiplin dan pengajaran Sufi.

Al-Ghazali menerima pendidikan awalnya di Tus kemudian di Jurjan sebelum datang ke Nishapur, di mana ia belajar di bawah Imam al-Haramayn al-Juwayni — teolog dan ahli fikih Syafi'i-Asy'ari terbesar pada eranya. Dari al-Juwayni ia menerima pelatihan menyeluruh dalam fikih Syafi'i maupun kalam Asy'ari. Setelah kematian al-Juwayni pada tahun 478 H, al-Ghazali berada di bawah naungan Nizam al-Mulk, wazir Seljuk yang kuat, yang menunjuknya untuk memimpin madrasah Nizamiyyah di Baghdad pada tahun 484 H.

Di Nizamiyyah al-Ghazali mencapai puncak kesuksesan ilmiah duniawi. Kuliah-kuliahnya menarik pendengar yang sangat banyak dan reputasinya tidak tertandingi. Namun pada tahun 488 H, pada puncak kemasyhurannya, ia meninggalkan posisinya dan pergi dari Baghdad — didorong, sebagaimana ia kemudian gambarkan dalam autobiografi spiritualnya Deliverance from Error (Al-Munqidh min ad-Dalal), oleh krisis spiritual yang mendalam tentang kepastian pengetahuan keagamaan. Ia menghabiskan bertahun-tahun di Suriah dan Palestina dalam khalwat Sufi sebelum akhirnya kembali mengajar di Khurasan.

Al-Mustasfa min Ilm al-Usul adalah salah satu karya-karya akhirnya, ditulis setelah kembali dari khalwat spiritual ketika ia telah mengintegrasikan pelatihan fikih awalnya dengan perspektif spiritual dan filosofis yang semakin mendalam. Pada titik ini ia juga telah menyelesaikan kritiknya yang dahsyat tentang filsafat (Tahafut al-Falasifah) dan mahakarya spiritualitas Islamnya (Ihya Ulum ad-Din). Al-Mustasfa mewakili kulminasi pemikirannya tentang fondasi-fondasi teoritis hukum Islam, ditulis dengan kedalaman dan kejelasan seorang ahli pada puncak kemampuannya. Al-Ghazali meninggal pada tahun 505 H (1111 M).
